expectations.text: Output file is named architecture-agentique.md (.md extension, not .txt)
Output uses Markdown headings/lists for the flowing text (title, explanatory bullets, 'TYPES DE RELATIONS' block)
expectations.evidence: '# Architecture agentique', '## Types de relations', '## Diagramme', '## Résumé' headings with nested bullet lists

expectations.text: The diagram section correctly states that Agent 1 (recherche générale) and Agent 2 (sources officielles) are launched in parallel by the Head Agent
expectations.evidence: '**PARALLÈLE** — lance deux agents en même temps, sans dépendance entre eux : **AGENT 1** — Recherche Générale / **AGENT 2** — Sources Officielles'

expectations.text: The diagram section correctly states that Agent 3 runs sequentially after Agent 1 and Agent 2 both finish, and that it synthesizes/combines their results — it must not present Agent 3 as a second parallel agent or duplicate the Agent 1/Agent 2 pair in a confusing second block
expectations.evidence: 'ne démarre qu'une fois Agent 1 et Agent 2 terminés ; combine leurs résultats' and inset reiterates 'SÉQUENTIEL → AGENT 3 Synthèse (lancé APRÈS 1 et 2)' — the second visual block (inset/legend) is explicitly labeled as a smaller-scale repetition of the same pattern, not presented as a separate ambiguous parallel agent

expectations.text: The Résumé section is consistent with the diagram section's flow (same parallel-then-sequential relationship), stated briefly
expectations.evidence: Résumé restates: 'lance d'abord deux agents en parallèle... Une fois que ces deux agents ont terminé, un troisième agent — Agent 3 — démarre... point de jonction/synthèse, donc séquentiel'

eval_feedback.overall: 5/5 pass. This is the first run (across both iterations) where the diagram's parallel/sequential logic is stated correctly and unambiguously in both the Diagramme and Résumé sections, with no confusing duplicate block.
